name: Balma de la Pola, Camí del Graons de Mura, Coll de Boix – Circular Route
name_original: Balma de la Pola, Camí del Graons de Mura, Coll de Boix – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Jogging routes around Mura traverse the rugged terrain of the Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac. This region is characterized by significant elevation changes, featuring numerous hills and cols, and dense woodlands. Runners can expect trails that wind through natural landscapes, offering a challenging experience with varied gradients. Best jogging routes around Mura The most popular jogging route is Balma de la Pola, Camí del Graons de Mura, Coll de Boix – Circular Route, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes about 45 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Morral del Drac (980 m) – La Mola (1,103 m) loop from Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac, a difficult 7.1 miles (11.5 km) path. This route offers panoramic views from two prominent peaks within the natural park. Local runners also love the Coll d'Eres loop from Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail leading through hilly forested areas, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Jogging around Mura is defined by its challenging ascents, forested paths, and scenic cols. The network offers options primarily for experienced runners seeking difficult trails. The routes in Mura are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from 1 review. More than 360 runners have used komoot to explore Mura's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Mura?
A: There are over 20 running routes around Mura, primarily located within the Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac. These routes offer a challenging experience with significant elevation changes and varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Mura?
A: Jogging routes in Mura traverse rugged terrain with numerous hills and cols, and dense woodlands. Runners should expect challenging ascents and varied gradients, making them suitable for experienced runners.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Mura?
A: Yes, Mura offers several circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964107978/balma-de-la-pola-cami-del-graons-de-mura-coll-de-boix-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balma de la Pola, Camí del Graons de Mura, Coll de Boix – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.1 miles (5.0 km) long and features significant elevation gain.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or landmarks along the running trails?
A: Many routes offer scenic views within the Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac. For panoramic vistas, consider routes that include peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/423435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola and the Monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/239333" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola (1,103 m)&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1475355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morral del Drac (980 m) – La Mola (1,103 m) loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically offers panoramic views from two prominent peaks.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes for beginners in Mura?
A: The jogging routes in Mura are primarily designed for experienced runners, with most being classified as 'difficult' due to challenging ascents and varied terrain. There are no routes specifically categorized as 'easy' in the guide data, and only one 'moderate' route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14981545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll d'Eres loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Mura?
A: The running routes in Mura are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars. Runners often praise the challenging nature of the trails, the forested paths, and the scenic cols within the Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac.

Q: Can I find any interesting natural features like caves along the running paths?
A: Yes, the region around Mura is known for its caves. While running, you might encounter natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2595230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morral del Drac&lt;/a&gt;, or explore areas near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2857414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cau dels Emboscats Cave&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2940326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sescorts Caves&lt;/a&gt;. For more details, you can check the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1656409/caves-around-mura" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caves around Mura guide&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for endurance training?
A: For longer runs, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/707938" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Boix (875 m) – Turó dels Ducs (820 m) loop from Mura&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 8.9 miles (14.3 km) and involves significant elevation gain, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10492302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porquerissa Cave – Coll de Tres Creus (872 m) loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.0 miles (11.3 km).

Q: What are the typical durations for running routes in Mura?
A: The duration of running routes in Mura varies depending on the distance and difficulty. For example, a 3.1-mile (5.0 km) difficult route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964107978/balma-de-la-pola-cami-del-graons-de-mura-coll-de-boix-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balma de la Pola, Camí del Graons de Mura, Coll de Boix – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; takes about 45 minutes, while a 7.1-mile (11.5 km) difficult path such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1475355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morral del Drac – La Mola loop&lt;/a&gt; can take over 1 hour 40 minutes.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the running trails?
A: Yes, the area offers several points of interest. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/423435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt&lt;/a&gt; at the summit of La Mola, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1847885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Agnès&lt;/a&gt;. For more historical context, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2867460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guanta Castle&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby. Explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1656404/attractions-around-mura" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Attractions around Mura guide&lt;/a&gt; for more details.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Mura?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations are not provided, the rugged terrain and dense woodlands suggest that spring and autumn might offer the most comfortable temperatures for challenging runs. Always check local weather conditions before heading out, especially given the significant elevation changes.
tips: Small signposted well where the GR-5 passes. From here, I recommend climbing the Cerro de Tres Creus or following the GR-5 towards Coll de Correu, going through the Paller de Tot l'Any, or towards Coll de Daví to climb Castellsapera.
It's funny, because at Christmas there are several cribs inside. There is a small cave.
Little hill near the Castellots de Tanca. I recommend following the path that passes through the Balma de la Pola to Coll de Tres Creus.
Great views towards Montserrat. We came from the direction of Alina del Sallari and were quite overwhelmed by the sudden wide view.

This cave is one of the many vestiges that remain from the passage of muleteers through the park. It was used as a shelter for the muleteers themselves and as a resting place with short stays for pack animals on their long journey to more distant places.
